info.title: ATS HRMS — Cloud Functions API
info.description:
HTTP endpoints exposed as Firebase Cloud Functions (onRequest). Callable functions (onCall) use the Firebase SDK protocol and are documented here for reference with their logical payload shapes.

Base URL pattern: `https://{region}-{project}.cloudfunctions.net/{functionName}`

Authentication: All protected endpoints require a Firebase ID token passed as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. In the local emulator the token `dev-recruiter` bypasses verification and resolves to uid `recruiter-dev`.

bearerAuth.description: Firebase Auth ID token. In the emulator, use `dev-recruiter` (recruiter) or `dev-candidate` (candidate) as the token value.

offerId.description: Present when the email attempt belongs to an offer letter

tokenHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the public token. The plain token is never persisted.

compensation.description: Overrides the compensation text. If omitted, job salary/currency defaults can be used.

benefits.description: Overrides job benefits. If omitted, defaults from the job are used.

publicUrl.description: Public secure link containing the plain token. Only tokenHash is stored in Firestore.

documentHtml.description: Generated offer letter HTML shown to the candidate.

SkillMatchStats.description: Weighted skill match statistics calculated by Cloud Function on application creation

changedBy.description: UID of the user who changed the stage

cvStoragePath.description: Firebase Storage path, e.g. cvs/candidateId/cv.pdf. Null if no CV uploaded.

CandidateProfileForConfirmation.description: Canonical candidate profile fields populated by manual input or CV parsing. All fields are optional because parsing can still be pending, processing, or failed.

EmailTemplateStage.description: Business-friendly stage key used to associate an email template with a recruiter pipeline stage

subject.description: Resolved subject — template variables already substituted

body.description: Resolved HTML body — template variables already substituted

tags.name: Jobs (Public)
tags.description: Public job listing endpoints — no authentication required

tags.name: Jobs (Internal)
tags.description: Internal position management — requires recruiter/admin role

tags.name: Auth / Admin
tags.description: User role assignment and employee provisioning

tags.name: Email Templates
tags.description: Gmail OAuth connection and automated candidate communication via email templates

get.description: Returns the current health status of the Cloud Functions service. No authentication required.
Returns all jobs with status `open`. No authentication required. Used by the candidate-facing job board.
Returns the full detail of a single job by its ID. No authentication required. Used by the candidate-facing job detail page.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Firestore document ID of the job

get.summary: List positions with filters and pagination
get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable list of positions. Requires authentication.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search term applied to title and department

get.description: Returns an array of unique department names across all jobs. Used to populate filter dropdowns.

get.summary: Get internal position detail by ID
get.description: Returns full job detail for internal recruiter use. Includes all fields. Requires authentication.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Firestore document ID of the position

post.description: Creates a new job position. The `hiringManagerId` is automatically set to the authenticated user's UID. Requires authentication.

id.description: Firestore document ID of the created job

patch.description: Partial update of a job position. The `id` field is required; all other fields are optional. Status and salary fields are excluded from this endpoint — use `updatePositionStatus` for status changes.

id.description: Firestore document ID of the position

patch.description: Dedicated endpoint for changing a position status (draft → open → paused → closed). Separated from `updatePosition` to enforce explicit status transitions.

id.description: Firestore document ID of the position

post.summary: Register a candidate with manual profile data
post.description: Registers a new candidate by submitting their profile data manually (no CV upload). Creates a `candidates` document and an `applications` document for the specified job. Returns 409 if the candidate is already registered for that job.

jobId.description: Firestore document ID of the target job

409.description: Candidate already registered for this job

post.description: Initializes a candidate registration flow via CV upload. Creates a `candidates` document with `cvParseStatus: pending` and returns an `uploadBasePath` in Firebase Storage where the frontend must upload the CV PDF. The `onCVUploaded` trigger then parses the CV and populates `parsedData`.

jobId.description: Firestore document ID of the target job

uploadBasePath.description: Firebase Storage base path where the CV must be uploaded, e.g. cvs/{candidateId}

get.summary: Get CV parsing status and preliminary candidate profile
get.description: Returns the current parsing status and canonical candidate profile fields for the CV confirmation screen. The frontend should poll this endpoint after uploading the CV until `cvParseStatus` becomes `done` or `failed`, then let the candidate review or complete the profile before calling `confirmCandidateProfile`.

403.description: Application does not belong to candidate

patch.summary: Confirm and complete a candidate's profile
patch.description: Called after the candidate reviews their AI-parsed data. Writes the confirmed profile fields to Firestore and transitions the candidate's `profileStatus` to `completed`. Optionally links to an existing application.

applicationId.description: Optional. Links the confirmation to an existing application.

post.summary: Submit an application for a job
post.description: Creates a new application linking the authenticated candidate to a job. The candidateId is derived from the auth token — it must not be passed in the body. Returns 409 if an application already exists for that candidate+job combination. Returns 422 if the job is not open.

jobId.description: Firestore document ID of the job to apply to

409.description: Application already exists for this candidate+job

422.description: Job is not open for applications

get.summary: Get all applications for a job
get.description: Returns all applications for a given job, optionally sorted and paginated. Each item includes denormalized candidate fields (name, email). Used to populate the recruiter pipeline board.

200.description: Array of applications with denormalized candidate data

patch.summary: Advance or change an application's stage
patch.description: Transitions an application to a new stage in the recruiter pipeline state machine. Writing to `rejected` requires `rejectionReason`. The `onStageChanged` Cloud Function trigger handles side effects (emails, employee migration on `hired`).
Partial update for recruiter-editable fields on an application. Currently supports updating `fortalezas` (candidate strengths noted by the recruiter).

fortalezas.description: List of candidate strengths as free-text strings

get.summary: Get the stage transition history of an application
get.description: Returns a chronological list of all stage transitions for an application, including who made each change and any associated notes or rejection reason.

get.summary: Get the Firebase Storage path for a candidate's CV
get.description: Returns the Firebase Storage path (`cvStoragePath`) for the CV associated with the given application. The frontend then calls `getDownloadURL(ref(storage, cvStoragePath))` to produce a signed download URL. Returns 404 if the application has no CV.

404.description: Application not found or candidate has no CV

post.description: Creates an Offer in `draft` status for an active, non-terminal application. Candidate/job data is denormalized, the offer letter HTML is generated from the default template and persisted in Storage. Compensation, modality and benefits can be overridden; otherwise job defaults are used when available.

409.description: Offer cannot be created for the current data state

put.description: Updates editable fields of an Offer in `draft` status, regenerates the stored HTML document and recalculates its hash.

409.description: Offer is not in draft status

post.description: Returns the generated HTML for an Offer in `draft` status without publishing it, generating a token or changing state.

409.description: Offer is not in draft status

post.summary: Publish/send an offer and generate a secure link
post.description: Generates a secure public link, resolves the configured `offer` email template, sends it through the recruiter's connected Gmail account, and records the attempt in emailLogs. Only after Gmail confirms delivery does it move the Offer from `draft` to `sent`, store the token hash, update the application to `stage: offer_sent`, and write stageHistory atomically.

200.description: Offer published and secure link generated

404.description: Offer or associated application not found

409.description: Offer is not in draft status or cannot be sent

get.summary: Get the latest offer linked to an application
get.description: Internal endpoint used by recruiter/manager dashboards to retrieve the latest Offer associated with an application. Returns `offer: null` when the application exists but no offer has been created yet.

200.description: Latest offer for the application, or null

get.summary: Get a public offer by token
get.description: Public endpoint used by candidates to retrieve a sent offer letter. The received token is hashed server-side and matched against `tokenHash`; expired links are marked as `expired`.

404.description: Offer not found or token is invalid

409.description: Offer is unavailable, already answered or expired

post.summary: Accept or decline a public offer
post.description: Public endpoint used by candidates to accept or decline a sent offer. It records response evidence including server timestamp, candidate email, signer name when accepting, IP, user agent and token usage. It updates only OfferStatus to `accepted` or `declined`; it does not mark the application as `hired` automatically.

404.description: Offer not found or token is invalid

409.description: Offer is unavailable, already answered or expired

post.summary: Provision an employee record for the authenticated user
post.description: Idempotent upsert: creates an `employees` document for the authenticated user if one does not already exist. Called automatically after Google SSO login. The document is created with `active: false` and `role: null` — an admin must call `setUserRole` to activate the account.

isNew.description: true if the document was just created, false if it already existed

post.summary: Assign a role to a user (admin only)
post.description: Sets Firebase Auth custom claims `{ role }` for the target user. Only callers with `role: admin` in their own token claims can call this endpoint. Returns 403 for non-admin callers.

uid.description: Firebase Auth UID of the target user

403.description: Caller does not have admin role

post.summary: Exchange a Google OAuth authorization code for Gmail credentials
post.description: Exchanges a Google OAuth2 authorization code (obtained via the `gmail.send` consent screen) for an access token and refresh token. Stores the credential in `users/{uid}.gmailCredential` so Cloud Functions can send emails on behalf of the authenticated user. Must be called once per user after the OAuth consent flow completes. Requires `access_type=offline&prompt=consent` in the OAuth request to obtain a refresh token.

code.description: Authorization code returned by Google OAuth2 consent screen

redirectUri.description: Must match exactly the redirect URI registered in the Google Cloud Console OAuth client

500.description: Google token exchange failed or internal error

get.summary: Get email communication history for a candidate
get.description: Returns all `EmailLog` records for the given candidate, ordered by `attemptedAt` descending. Used to populate the Comunicaciones section in the candidate profile and the global email history view. Logs with `status=failed` include an `error` field explaining the failure.

parameters.name: candidateId
parameters.description: Firestore document ID of the candidate

200.description: Email log entries for the candidate

post.description: Re-attempts sending an email for a log entry with `status=failed` or `status=pending`. Uses the already-resolved `subject` and `body` stored in the log (no re-resolution of template variables). The email is sent using the Gmail credential of the authenticated user making the retry request. Updates `status` to `sent` or `failed` accordingly.

logId.description: Firestore document ID of the EmailLog to retry

500.description: Gmail send failed or internal error

x-callable-functions.description: The following functions use the Firebase onCall protocol (not plain HTTP). They are invoked via the Firebase client SDK (`httpsCallable`) and are not directly callable with curl/fetch. They are documented here for completeness.

functions.name: getApplicationsByCandidate
functions.description: Returns all applications for a given candidate. Optionally filtered by jobId.

request.jobId: string (optional) — if provided, returns only the application for that job

functions.name: getApplicationDetail
functions.description: Returns the full detail of a single application, including denormalized candidate and job data.

functions.name: getInternalJobDetail
functions.description: Returns the full Job object for internal recruiter use.
